tools.name: configure
tools.description: Inject the API key the app needs before it serves traffic. The runtime keeps every other export frozen until this returns Ok, then lifts the gate automatically (the app does not call set-config-complete). The key is persisted to the per-app sealed KV store and SHA-256(key) is committed to the RA-TLS leaf at OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.65230.3.5.1.

tools.name: protected-call
tools.description: Returns the configured API key length once the app has been configured. Frozen (awaiting initial configuration) until configure succeeds.

tools.name: kv-store
tools.description: Write a value to the per-app sealed KV store.

tools.name: kv-read
tools.description: Read a value previously written with kv-store.

store.tagline: The WASM starter app plus typed config + actions: inject a secret at deploy time, attested and gated, with the gate owned by the runtime.
store.keywords: wasm, webassembly, example, configure, freeze gate, actions, secret injection, ra-tls, attestation, enclave os, sgx, confidential computing, sealed storage, mcp
store.description:
WASM Example (with config) is the reference application for the typed config + actions capabilities on the Privasys WASM runtime. It does everything the basic WASM Example does (sealed storage, hardware randomness, attestable HTTPS egress, a typed MCP tool, authenticated and role-gated endpoints) and adds the configure-then-freeze pattern.

CONFIGURE-THEN-FREEZE (runtime-gated)
The manifest tags the configure export role:config. On deploy the runtime keeps every other export frozen until configure returns Ok, then lifts the gate automatically. The app contains NO freeze logic and does NOT call set-config-complete: the gate is owned by the runtime/routing layer. The gate re-arms on every enclave restart.

configure persists the secret to the per-app sealed KV store, hashes it, and advertises the hash on the per-app RA-TLS leaf so verifying clients can prove the running app saw exactly the delivered key without seeing it.

WHO IT IS FOR
Developers who need to provision a secret or trust anchor into an enclave app at deploy time and have that provisioning attested.
